lab.title: Lab 01 - Manage Microsoft Entra ID Identities

steps.title: Sign in to Azure Portal
steps.instruction: Sign in to the Azure portal at https://portal.azure.com and dismiss the Welcome splash screen by selecting Cancel
steps.aiResponse:
I don't have browser automation tools available in this session. I can't navigate to URLs, click UI elements, or take screenshots — those capabilities require the **Playwright MCP server** that the lab-drift-runner pipeline uses.

To execute this step, you'd run it through the runner:

```bash
npm run dev -- scan https://portal.azure.com
```

Or if you want me to help with something else — like modifying the runner code, generating specs, or analyzing existing drift reports — I'm happy to help with that.

driftIssues.instructionText: Sign in to the Azure portal at https://portal.azure.com and dismiss the Welcome splash screen by selecting Cancel
driftIssues.actualBehavior: Element not found
driftIssues.recommendation: Update lab instructions - element may have been removed or renamed

steps.title: Search for and select Microsoft Entra ID
steps.instruction: Search for and select Microsoft Entra ID to access Azure's cloud-based identity and access management solution
steps.aiResponse:
As I mentioned, I don't have browser automation capabilities in this session. I cannot:

- Navigate web pages
- Click UI elements or search in the Azure portal
- Take screenshots
- Interact with Microsoft Entra ID or any web application

These lab steps require the **Playwright MCP server** which is configured when the runner pipeline (`runner-mcp-drift.ts`) creates a Copilot SDK session.

**What I can help with:**
- Modifying runner/drift detection code
- Creating or editing lab specs
- Analyzing existing reports in `reports/`
- Debugging the pipeline
- Any code/file tasks in this repo

Would you like help with any of those instead?

driftIssues.actualBehavior: Element not found

steps.title: Explore Overview and Manage tenants
steps.instruction: Select the Overview blade and then the Manage tenants tab to view tenant information, then return to the Entra ID page
steps.aiResponse:
I cannot execute browser-based lab steps. I don't have Playwright MCP or any browser automation tools in this session.

It looks like these step instructions are being sent to me repeatedly. If you're building/debugging the runner that sends these prompts, the issue is that **the execution target needs to be a Copilot SDK session with Playwright MCP configured**, not this CLI assistant.

If you need help fixing the routing so steps go to the right executor, I can look at the runner code. Just let me know.
